raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/year> I placed an order on XXXX a merchant I frequently have done business with. They did not deliver any of my items and I spoke to them on the phone and I thought it was all resolved. I believed they would act in good faith as I have done business with them plenty of times prior and they emailed me with a refund notification which I had provided to Amex with my dispute. Upon not receiving the refund and constant delays according to the merchant I decided to chargeback the amount and was left with the charge somehow. The merchant started to refuse to refund me even after promising me the refund. Amex upon seeing the refund email sided with the merchant on basically them pleading to them to not refund me. This is unacceptable, I have spent thousands of dollars with Amex and they see the merchant promised to refund me and still wont pull the credit from them. The merchant no longer responds to me and Amex had initially sided with me however upon them appealing and stating and I quote PLEASE DO NOT DEBIT OUR ACCOUNT AS THE ITEMS HAVE BEEN DELIVERED TO THE CUSTOMER 'S ADDRESS AND SIGNED FOR AS SHOWN ON THE PROOF OF DELIVERY. A CREDIT IS NOT DUE TO THE CUSTOMER. decided to overturn my credit. This is baseless and a blatant lie as they promised to refund me with the notification. The merchant assured me the packages are insured and I would receive the refund. I can not believe upon simply pleading from the merchants part, Im on the hook for {$3700.00} this is bad acting on both the merchant and Amex and Amex should be having my back as a customer as they promised to refund me. Amex states that the merchant never promised to refund me. I clearly showed the email that they sent to me. They told me I need to return the merchandise to recieved credit but I never recieved the merchandise so how is that possible. I am attaching the email they provided for the refund which is clearly from them and the order confirmation. I expect a swift response and refund from this as it has taken far too long to resolve.
